questions.question: What is a video and photo retainer?
questions.answer: A retainer is an ongoing partnership where we provide a set amount of video and photo production each month. Instead of booking project-by-project, you get priority scheduling, consistent content output, and typically a lower per-deliverable cost. It's ideal for businesses that need regular content for social media, events, or marketing campaigns.

questions.question: How does pricing work for a monthly retainer?
questions.answer: Retainer pricing is based on the scope of deliverables you need each month—number of shoots, video edits, photos, and turnaround time. We build a custom package that fits your budget and content goals. Most retainers start at a monthly commitment with flexible terms after an initial period.

questions.question: What's included in a typical retainer package?
questions.answer: Packages vary, but most include a set number of shoot days, edited videos (short-form and/or long-form), edited photos, and revisions. We also include content strategy sessions to plan your calendar and ensure every shoot aligns with your marketing goals.

questions.question: How is a retainer different from hiring us per project?
questions.answer: Per-project work is great for one-time needs, but retainers give you consistency, priority booking, and cost savings. You also get a production team that deeply understands your brand voice—no re-explaining your style every time.

questions.question: Can I adjust my retainer if my needs change?
questions.answer: Yes. We review retainer scopes quarterly and can scale up or down based on your business needs. If you have a big event coming up, we can add capacity. If things slow down, we can adjust accordingly.

questions.question: What types of businesses benefit most from a retainer?
questions.answer: Businesses with ongoing content needs—venues, restaurants, event spaces, real estate teams, and brands running active social media or ad campaigns. If you're constantly scrambling for fresh content, a retainer takes that off your plate.
